title: oplismenus hirtellus subsp. japonicus
intro: Oplismenus hirtellus subsp. japonicus, also known as Japanese false manna grass, is a type of grass native to Japan and parts of East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions, as well as having antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Oplismenus hirtellus subsp. japonicus is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
